relationship.description: Each CODE belongs to exactly one CODE_SYSTEM (CODE.system_id → CODE_SYSTEM.system_id)
Each CODE_RELATIONSHIP uses one RELATIONSHIP_TYPE (CODE_RELATIONSHIP.type_id → RELATIONSHIP_TYPE.type_id)
A CODE can appear as the source in many CODE_RELATIONSHIP records (CODE_RELATIONSHIP.source_code_id → CODE.code_id)
A CODE can appear as the target in many CODE_RELATIONSHIP records (CODE_RELATIONSHIP.target_code_id → CODE.code_id)
Each CODE_RELATIONSHIP may have multiple AUDIT_LOG entries documenting changes (AUDIT_LOG.rel_id → CODE_RELATIONSHIP.rel_id)

metadata.improvements_made: Replaced the derived unique_constraint_hash with a natural composite UNIQUE constraint on (source_code_id, target_code_id, type_id) to enforce relationship uniqueness directly in the DB. Removed the unnecessary hash attribute from CODE_RELATIONSHIP. Added explicit foreign‑key descriptions in the relationships to guarantee referential integrity. Retained the RELATIONSHIP_TYPE lookup for extensibility and kept the multi‑row AUDIT_LOG for full change history. Clarified source vs. target role in CODE‑CODE_RELATIONSHIP relationships. All original strengths (time validity, audit, code system hierarchy) are preserved.
